title: castanopsis costata
intro: Castanopsis costata, also known as the Taiwan chinquapin or Taiwanese chestnut oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Taiwan. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Castanopsis costata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Castanopsis costata are typically made by steaming the bark, leaves, or roots to create infusions, teas, or decoctions for medicinal use.
